<explanation>
The `__aexit__` method for the `ThreadSensitiveContext` class needs to reset the context variable by resetting the token if it was set during `__aenter__`. This ensures that the thread-sensitive context is properly cleaned up when exiting the context manager. The implementation should check if `self.token` is not None and then reset the context variable using the token.
</explanation>

<explanation>
The `__aexit__` method for the `ThreadSensitiveContext` class needs to reset the context variable by resetting the token if it was set during `__aenter__`. This ensures that the thread-sensitive context is properly cleaned up when exiting the context manager. The implementation should check if `self.token` is not None and then reset the context variable using the token.
</explanation>

```
async def __aexit__ (self, exc, value, tb) :
    if self.token is not None:
        SyncToAsync.thread_sensitive_context.reset(self.token)
```
